name: structural-wall-crack
description: There is a diagonal crack in the plaster on my living room wall. It's been there since I moved in 2 years ago.

hypotheses.label: Seasonal movement (non-structural)
evidence_for: Diagonal crack pattern typical of thermal movement

hypotheses.label: Foundation settlement
evidence_for: Diagonal crack can indicate differential settlement
evidence_against: 2-year-old crack that hasn't grown suggests stable

hypotheses.label: Lintel deflection over opening
evidence_for: Diagonal crack near opening consistent with lintel failure
evidence_against: No mention of a door or window nearby

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: We cannot tell whether the crack is growing or has been completely static since it appeared.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: A growing crack is a structural warning sign; a static crack over 2+ years is almost always cosmetic seasonal movement.

chips.text: It looks the same as when I first noticed it.
It has grown or new cracks appeared nearby.
It is right next to a door or window.

expected.must_ask_about: whether the crack is growing or static
